newsSinceJun6: June 9, 2026: Keppel announced its private fund Keppel Data Centre Fund III secured land (73% effective stake in the SPV, alongside Shinyoung, NH Securities and Hyundai E&C) to develop a 60MW AI-ready, Tier III-equivalent greenfield data centre in Ansan, Seoul Metropolitan Area - Keppel's first entry into the South Korean data centre market. Construction permits and power approvals are already secured; ready-for-service targeted 2030. Adds to a portfolio of 39 data centres with 800MW+ gross capacity at end-2025. Stock reaction muted (+0.48% on the day, +1.35% YTD), so the new evidence arrives without a rerating.

sources.label: Reuters via TradingView - KDCF III secures Ansan 60MW greenfield land
DataCenterDynamics - Keppel enters South Korean DC market, 60MW Seoul-area facility

verdicts.rankRationale: Genuinely new on-thesis evidence after yesterday's verdict: the June 9 Ansan announcement extends Keppel's data-center development pipeline into a new market with power approvals already in hand - exactly the physical-deployment bottleneck exposure this page rewards - while the stock remains un-rerated (+1.35% YTD), so residual alpha improves rather than erodes.
verdicts.bull: Secured power and permits in the supply-constrained Seoul Metropolitan Area is scarce bottleneck capacity; Keppel's fund model lets it scale AI-ready megawatts (Ansan, KDC SGP 9, floating DC) asset-light, and none of this is priced in at a flat YTD tape.
verdicts.neutral: The Ansan project is 60MW at a ~73% fund-level stake with RFS in 2030 - incremental (~7.5% of existing 800MW portfolio) and slow-burn for a USD ~15B conglomerate, so near-term earnings impact is modest even if strategically on-thesis.
verdicts.bear: Keppel is a diversified conglomerate, so data-center upside is diluted by legacy infrastructure and real-estate exposure; Korea DC development carries execution, power-cost and 2030-vintage demand risk, and fund structures cap Keppel's direct economics.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Further AI-ready capacity announcements and monetization events (KDC SGP 9 construction start mid-2026, additional fund deployments or REIT divestments at premium valuations).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Execution/timeline slippage on greenfield projects and conglomerate dilution - data-center wins fail to move group earnings enough to rerate the stock.
